title: Golden Crisp Dutch Bryndza Cheese Roast
description: A peppery and crisp festive Dutch dish featuring bryndza cheese and roast, friendly to ayurvedic tamasic diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the beef roast with black pepper and place it in a roasting pan.
Roast the beef in the oven for 20 minutes per pound, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the beef is roasting, toss the onion, carrot, and potato with olive oil, and spread them out in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast the vegetables in the oven for 30 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
Once the beef is done, let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it thinly.
To serve, place sliced beef on a plate and top it with crumbled bryndza cheese and roasted vegetables.
Garnish with fresh herbs, such as parsley or thyme, if desired.
